Transformation digitale et performance boursiere Modelisation econometriques

BOUKHIT KHALID

2025-05-22

==============================================================================

Digital Transformation and Stock Market Performance

Author: BOUKHIT KHALID (2025)

Contact: khalid.boukhit01@gmail.com

==============================================================================

<– Section: Setup –>

# library
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr); library(FactoMineR); library(ggplot2); library(dplyr)
  library(explor); library(plm); library(ggrepel); library(scales)
  library(car); library(plotly); library(lme4); library(sampleSelection)
  library(lmtest); library(sandwich); library(censReg); library(lfe)
  library(stargazer); library(quantreg); library(dynlm); library(ivreg)
  library(caret); library(AER); library(mediation); library(nnet)
  library(rdrobust); library(mgcv); library(survival); library(survminer)
  library(interactions); library(broom); library(viridis)
})

<– Section: Load Data –>

# Data 
TP <- read_csv(file.choose())
## Rows: 96 Columns: 31
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (4): id, Secteur, constitution, IPO
## dbl (27): year, collaborateurs, Age, CapitalSocial, CapitauxPropres, NombreT...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
TP$Secteur <- as.factor(TP$Secteur)
TP$year <- as.factor(TP$year)

<– Section: Digital Maturity Index –>

# indice composiste MD
dfMD <- TP[, c("id", "year", "engagementDirection", "Competences", "Automatisation", "AdoptionTech", "CoutsOperationnels", "InvestissementDigital")]
res.pca <- PCA(dfMD[,3:8], graph = FALSE)
res.pca$eig
##        eigenvalue percentage of variance cumulative percentage of variance
## comp 1 5.49626594             91.6044324                          91.60443
## comp 2 0.19685748              3.2809580                          94.88539
## comp 3 0.13173162              2.1955271                          97.08092
## comp 4 0.08083939              1.3473232                          98.42824
## comp 5 0.05773897              0.9623161                          99.39056
## comp 6 0.03656660              0.6094434                         100.00000
TP$MD <- res.pca$ind$coord[,1]
TP <- TP %>%mutate(MD = scales::rescale(MD, to = c(0, 100)))
ggplot(TP, aes(x = MD)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "blue", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Distribution de l'indice de maturite digitale",
       x = "Indice de maturite digitale",
       y = "Nombre d'entreprises")

<– Section: Stock Market Performance Index –>

# indice composiste PB
dfPB <- TP[, c("id", "year", "CapitalSocial", "CapitauxPropres", "CA", "BPA", "ROE", "DividendYield", "PER", "ROA")]
res.pca.performance <- PCA(dfPB[, 3:10], graph = FALSE)
SOMMECUMULE <- cumsum(res.pca.performance$eig[, "percentage of variance"])   # le pourcentage cumulé de variance expliquée
nbcomposante <- which(SOMMECUMULE >= 80)[1]
cat("Nombre de composantes necessaires pour expliquer au moins 80 % de la variance :", nbcomposante, "\n")
## Nombre de composantes necessaires pour expliquer au moins 80 % de la variance : 5
# les poids basés sur la variance expliquée
poid <- res.pca.performance$eig[1:nbcomposante, "percentage of variance"] / sum(res.pca.performance$eig[1:nbcomposante, "percentage of variance"]) 
TP$PB <- as.vector(res.pca.performance$ind$coord[, 1:nbcomposante] %*% poid) # Calcul de l'indicateur composite comme une somme pondérée des scores
TP <- TP %>% mutate(PB = scales::rescale(PB, to = c(0, 100)))

<– Section: Descriptive Statistics –>

# statistiques 
summary(TP$MD); summary(TP$PB)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##    0.00   25.68   51.96   51.39   77.84  100.00
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##    0.00   24.27   31.29   36.74   44.65  100.00
mean(TP$MD); mean(TP$PB); sd(TP$MD); sd(TP$PB)
## [1] 51.38972
## [1] 36.74481
## [1] 30.49954
## [1] 20.56822
r <- cor(TP$PB,TP$MD, method = "pearson"); print(paste("le coeff de correlation est",r))
## [1] "le coeff de correlation est 0.346660539590902"
summary(TP)
##       id              year   
##  Length:96          2021:32  
##  Class :character   2022:32  
##  Mode  :character   2023:32  
##                              
##                              
##                              
##                              
##                                            Secteur   collaborateurs  
##  Agroalimentaire et Production                 : 6   Min.   :  44.0  
##  Assurances                                    : 6   1st Qu.: 426.2  
##  Banques                                       : 6   Median : 966.5  
##  Batiment et Materiaux de Construction         : 6   Mean   :1797.0  
##  Distributeurs                                 : 6   3rd Qu.:2190.8  
##  Matériels, Logiciels et Services Informatiques: 6   Max.   :8480.0  
##  (Other)                                       :60                   
##  constitution            Age             IPO            CapitalSocial      
##  Length:96          Min.   :  7.00   Length:96          Min.   :1.457e+07  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.: 34.25   Class :character   1st Qu.:1.980e+08  
##  Mode  :character   Median : 50.00   Mode  :character   Median :4.040e+08  
##                     Mean   : 57.69                      Mean   :9.563e+08  
##                     3rd Qu.: 86.50                      3rd Qu.:1.199e+09  
##                     Max.   :114.00                      Max.   :5.275e+09  
##                                                                            
##  CapitauxPropres       NombreTitres       ValeurNominale          CA           
##  Min.   :-3.288e+08   Min.   :   291500   Min.   :  10.00   Min.   :3.986e+07  
##  1st Qu.: 7.540e+08   1st Qu.:  2400000   1st Qu.:  10.00   1st Qu.:6.434e+08  
##  Median : 1.897e+09   Median :  9991308   Median :  50.00   Median :2.249e+09  
##  Mean   : 5.078e+09   Mean   : 64348115   Mean   :  75.84   Mean   :5.702e+09  
##  3rd Qu.: 4.779e+09   3rd Qu.: 35463470   3rd Qu.: 100.00   3rd Qu.:7.028e+09  
##  Max.   : 6.671e+10   Max.   :879095340   Max.   :1000.00   Max.   :3.679e+10  
##                                                                                
##  ResultatExploitation  ResultatNet              BPA              ROE        
##  Min.   :-1.276e+08   Min.   :-172000000   Min.   :-32.00   Min.   :-13.00  
##  1st Qu.: 1.721e+08   1st Qu.:  80699607   1st Qu.:  7.00   1st Qu.:  8.00  
##  Median : 3.105e+08   Median : 173375686   Median : 24.50   Median : 12.00  
##  Mean   : 1.317e+09   Mean   : 641429639   Mean   : 41.05   Mean   : 13.52  
##  3rd Qu.: 1.050e+09   3rd Qu.: 669439250   3rd Qu.: 51.50   3rd Qu.: 18.00  
##  Max.   : 1.775e+10   Max.   :7507605000   Max.   :259.00   Max.   : 40.00  
##                                                                             
##      Payout        DividendYield        PER               PBR       
##  Min.   :   0.00   Min.   :0.000   Min.   : -41.00   Min.   :0.000  
##  1st Qu.:  37.75   1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.:  15.00   1st Qu.:2.000  
##  Median :  63.50   Median :3.000   Median :  19.00   Median :3.000  
##  Mean   :  99.10   Mean   :2.802   Mean   :  63.16   Mean   :3.216  
##  3rd Qu.:  80.00   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:  32.00   3rd Qu.:4.000  
##  Max.   :2763.00   Max.   :8.000   Max.   :3730.00   Max.   :9.000  
##                                                      NA's   :8      
##    Dividendes     Capitalisation        ROA          ResponsableDigitale
##  Min.   :  0.00   Min.   :    34   Min.   :-10.000   Min.   :0.000      
##  1st Qu.:  4.00   1st Qu.:  1884   1st Qu.:  2.000   1st Qu.:0.000      
##  Median : 14.00   Median :  4592   Median :  4.000   Median :1.000      
##  Mean   : 24.24   Mean   : 13211   Mean   :  5.656   Mean   :0.625      
##  3rd Qu.: 30.00   3rd Qu.: 14336   3rd Qu.:  7.250   3rd Qu.:1.000      
##  Max.   :140.00   Max.   :107250   Max.   : 77.000   Max.   :1.000      
##                   NA's   :3                                             
##  engagementDirection  Competences    Automatisation   AdoptionTech  
##  Min.   :1.000       Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:2.000       1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000  
##  Median :4.000       Median :3.000   Median :3.000   Median :3.000  
##  Mean   :3.438       Mean   :2.896   Mean   :2.969   Mean   :3.042  
##  3rd Qu.:5.000       3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:4.000  
##  Max.   :5.000       Max.   :5.000   Max.   :5.000   Max.   :5.000  
##                                                                     
##  CoutsOperationnels  Concurrence    InvestissementDigital       MD        
##  Min.   :1.000      Min.   :2.000   Min.   :1.000         Min.   :  0.00  
##  1st Qu.:1.000      1st Qu.:3.000   1st Qu.:2.000         1st Qu.: 25.68  
##  Median :2.000      Median :4.000   Median :3.000         Median : 51.96  
##  Mean   :2.448      Mean   :3.688   Mean   :3.083         Mean   : 51.39  
##  3rd Qu.:3.000      3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:4.000         3rd Qu.: 77.84  
##  Max.   :5.000      Max.   :5.000   Max.   :5.000         Max.   :100.00  
##                                                                           
##        PB        
##  Min.   :  0.00  
##  1st Qu.: 24.27  
##  Median : 31.29  
##  Mean   : 36.74  
##  3rd Qu.: 44.65  
##  Max.   :100.00  
## 
hist(TP$Age, main="Distribution de l'age", xlab="Age")

hist(TP$collaborateurs, main="Distribution des collaborateurs", xlab="Collaborateurs")

hist(TP$CA, main="Distribution du chiffre d'affaires", xlab="Chiffre d'affaires")

hist(TP$ResponsableDigitale, main="Distribution des responsables digitaux", xlab="Presence d un Responsable Digital")

<– Section: Panel Data Setup –>

pdata <- pdata.frame(TP)
pdatana <- na.omit(pdata)

<– Hypothesis H1 –>

# H1 : Les entreprises cotées ayant une maturité digitale élevée génèreraient de meilleures performances boursières que celles dont 
# la maturité digitale est faible.
rg <- lm(PB ~ MD, TP);summary(rg)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PB ~ MD, data = TP)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -27.649 -11.753  -4.215   6.785  58.087 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 24.73091    3.89359   6.352 7.52e-09 ***
## MD           0.23378    0.06524   3.583  0.00054 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 19.4 on 94 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1202, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1108 
## F-statistic: 12.84 on 1 and 94 DF,  p-value: 0.0005401
par(mfrow = c(2, 2)); plot(rg)

residus <- residuals(rg); qq <- data.frame(residus = residus)
ggplot(qq, aes(sample = residus)) + stat_qq() + stat_qq_line(col = "red") + ggtitle("QQ Plot des Résidus") + theme_minimal() #  QQ plot

shapiro.test(resid(rg))  # Test de normalité des résidus avec Shapiro-Wilk
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  resid(rg)
## W = 0.91592, p-value = 1.259e-05
bptest(rg)  # test de Breusch-Pagan
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  rg
## BP = 3.1854, df = 1, p-value = 0.0743
fitted_values <- fitted(rg) ; std_residuals <- sqrt(abs(scale(residuals(rg))))
scalloc <- data.frame(Fitted = fitted_values, StdResiduals = std_residuals)
# Graphique Scale-Location
ggplot(scalloc, aes(x = Fitted, y = StdResiduals)) + geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("Graphique Scale-Location des Résidus") + xlab("Valeurs ajustées") + ylab("√(|Résidus standardisés|)") +
  theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

durbinWatsonTest(rg) # test Durbin-Watson
##  lag Autocorrelation D-W Statistic p-value
##    1       0.5396569     0.9110851       0
##  Alternative hypothesis: rho != 0
# regression panel
Reg.pH1 <- plm(PB ~ MD, pdata, model = "pooling"); summary(Reg.pH1)
## Pooling Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD, data = pdata, model = "pooling")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -27.6494 -11.7530  -4.2154   6.7852  58.0867 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t-value  Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 24.730915   3.893592  6.3517  7.52e-09 ***
## MD           0.233780   0.065244  3.5832 0.0005401 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    40190
## Residual Sum of Squares: 35360
## R-Squared:      0.12017
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.11081
## F-statistic: 12.8392 on 1 and 94 DF, p-value: 0.00054014
Reg.bH1 <- update(Reg.pH1, model = "between"); summary(Reg.bH1)
## Oneway (individual) effect Between Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD, data = pdata, model = "between")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## Observations used in estimation: 32
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -24.5440 -12.2784  -1.9712   2.3589  51.8561 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept) 22.54551    6.92688  3.2548 0.002811 **
## MD           0.27631    0.11914  2.3191 0.027387 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    11879
## Residual Sum of Squares: 10073
## R-Squared:      0.15202
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.12375
## F-statistic: 5.37812 on 1 and 30 DF, p-value: 0.027387
Reg.wH1 <- update(Reg.pH1, model = "within"); summary(Reg.wH1)
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD, data = pdata, model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## -24.90961  -2.15753  -0.34271   2.55465  25.52752 
## 
## Coefficients:
##    Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)
## MD 0.060475   0.063977  0.9453   0.3481
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    4553.6
## Residual Sum of Squares: 4489.9
## R-Squared:      0.013985
## Adj. R-Squared: -0.48685
## F-statistic: 0.893544 on 1 and 63 DF, p-value: 0.34813
Reg.rH1 <- update(Reg.pH1, model = "random"); summary(Reg.rH1)
## Oneway (individual) effect Random Effect Model 
##    (Swamy-Arora's transformation)
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD, data = pdata, model = "random")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Effects:
##                   var std.dev share
## idiosyncratic  71.268   8.442 0.186
## individual    312.010  17.664 0.814
## theta: 0.734
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -24.1723  -4.5426  -1.3675   2.6589  26.8786 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error z-value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept) 31.154704   4.381048  7.1112 1.15e-12 ***
## MD           0.108779   0.056827  1.9142  0.05559 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    7074.9
## Residual Sum of Squares: 6809.4
## R-Squared:      0.037519
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.02728
## Chisq: 3.66424 on 1 DF, p-value: 0.055592
pFtest(Reg.wH1, Reg.pH1) # test de Fisher
## 
##  F test for individual effects
## 
## data:  PB ~ MD
## F = 13.973, df1 = 31, df2 = 63, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: significant effects
phtest(Reg.wH1, Reg.rH1) # test de hausman
## 
##  Hausman Test
## 
## data:  PB ~ MD
## chisq = 2.7013, df = 1, p-value = 0.1003
## alternative hypothesis: one model is inconsistent
resid <- residuals(Reg.pH1); shapiro.test(resid)  # Normalité
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  resid
## W = 0.91592, p-value = 1.259e-05
bptest(Reg.pH1)     # Hétéroscédasticité
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  Reg.pH1
## BP = 3.1854, df = 1, p-value = 0.0743
pwartest(Reg.wH1)   # Autocorrélation
## 
##  Wooldridge's test for serial correlation in FE panels
## 
## data:  Reg.wH1
## F = 1.8666, df1 = 1, df2 = 62, p-value = 0.1768
## alternative hypothesis: serial correlation
poole <- plm(log(PB+0.01) ~ log(MD+0.01), pdata,model = "pooling"); summary(poole)
## Pooling Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = log(PB + 0.01) ~ log(MD + 0.01), data = pdata, 
##     model = "pooling")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## -7.116116 -0.265506  0.040452  0.480164  1.528613 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                Estimate Std. Error t-value  Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)    3.038643   0.144902 20.9704 < 2.2e-16 ***
## log(MD + 0.01) 0.114588   0.035792  3.2015  0.001866 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    98.327
## Residual Sum of Squares: 88.66
## R-Squared:      0.098318
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.088725
## F-statistic: 10.2496 on 1 and 94 DF, p-value: 0.0018657
withh <- plm(log(PB+0.01) ~ log(MD+0.01), pdata,model = "within"); summary(withh)
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = log(PB + 0.01) ~ log(MD + 0.01), data = pdata, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min.    1st Qu.     Median    3rd Qu.       Max. 
## -5.2926033 -0.0666915  0.0056693  0.0821405  2.7558226 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)
## log(MD + 0.01) 0.0090065  0.0776895  0.1159   0.9081
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    46.344
## Residual Sum of Squares: 46.334
## R-Squared:      0.00021328
## Adj. R-Squared: -0.50761
## F-statistic: 0.0134395 on 1 and 63 DF, p-value: 0.90808
pFtest(withh, poole)
## 
##  F test for individual effects
## 
## data:  log(PB + 0.01) ~ log(MD + 0.01)
## F = 1.8565, df1 = 31, df2 = 63, p-value = 0.01901
## alternative hypothesis: significant effects
regtobitH1 <- censReg(PB ~ MD , data = pdata); summary(regtobitH1) # regression Tobit
## 
## Call:
## censReg(formula = PB ~ MD, data = pdata)
## 
## Observations:
##          Total  Left-censored     Uncensored Right-censored 
##             96              1             95              0 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. error t value Pr(> t)    
## (Intercept) 32.37054    2.58363  12.529  <2e-16 ***
## MD           0.07746    0.04605   1.682  0.0926 .  
## logSigmaMu   2.84508    0.09884  28.784  <2e-16 ***
## logSigmaNu   2.18952    0.07790  28.108  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Newton-Raphson maximisation, 6 iterations
## Return code 1: gradient close to zero (gradtol)
## Log-likelihood: -381.6084 on 4 Df
dynamicH1 <- dynlm(PB ~ L(MD, 1), data = pdata); summary(dynamicH1) # Modèle dynamique avec décalage temporel
## 
## Time series regression with "numeric" data:
## Start = 1, End = 96
## 
## Call:
## dynlm(formula = PB ~ L(MD, 1), data = pdata)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -27.649 -11.753  -4.215   6.785  58.087 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 24.73091    3.89359   6.352 7.52e-09 ***
## L(MD, 1)     0.23378    0.06524   3.583  0.00054 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 19.4 on 94 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1202, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1108 
## F-statistic: 12.84 on 1 and 94 DF,  p-value: 0.0005401
trainingH1 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)
moddedH1 <- train(PB ~ MD, data = pdata, method = "lm", trControl = trainingH1); summary(moddedH1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = .outcome ~ ., data = dat)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -27.649 -11.753  -4.215   6.785  58.087 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 24.73091    3.89359   6.352 7.52e-09 ***
## MD           0.23378    0.06524   3.583  0.00054 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 19.4 on 94 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1202, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1108 
## F-statistic: 12.84 on 1 and 94 DF,  p-value: 0.0005401
modelH1IV <- ivreg(PB ~ MD | lag(MD), data = pdata); summary(modelH1IV) # Régression IV
## 
## Call:
## ivreg(formula = PB ~ MD | lag(MD), data = pdata)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -27.649 -11.753  -4.215   6.785  58.087 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 24.73091    3.89359   6.352 7.52e-09 ***
## MD           0.23378    0.06524   3.583  0.00054 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 19.4 on 94 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-Squared: 0.1202,  Adjusted R-squared: 0.1108 
## Wald test: 12.84 on 1 and 94 DF,  p-value: 0.0005401
first_stage <- lm(MD ~ lag(MD), data = TP); fs_fstat <- summary(first_stage)$fstatistic[1]
## Warning in summary.lm(first_stage): essentially perfect fit: summary may be
## unreliable
names(fs_fstat) <- "First-stage F-statistic"; print(fs_fstat)
## First-stage F-statistic 
##            6.490817e+32
pdata <- pdata %>% group_by(id) %>% mutate(MD_mean = mean(MD)) %>% ungroup() # mundlak model
mundlak_model <- plm(PB ~ MD + MD_mean, data = pdata, model = "random"); summary(mundlak_model)
## Oneway (individual) effect Random Effect Model 
##    (Swamy-Arora's transformation)
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + MD_mean, data = pdata, model = "random")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Effects:
##                   var std.dev share
## idiosyncratic  71.268   8.442 0.186
## individual    312.010  17.664 0.814
## theta: 0.734
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -25.9451  -4.7135  -1.2732   2.8675  24.4920 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error z-value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept) 22.545515   6.926880  3.2548 0.001135 **
## MD           0.060475   0.063977  0.9453 0.344519   
## MD_mean      0.215831   0.135235  1.5960 0.110496   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    7074.9
## Residual Sum of Squares: 6627.9
## R-Squared:      0.063177
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.04303
## Chisq: 6.27166 on 2 DF, p-value: 0.043464
gam_model <- gam(PB ~ s(MD) + MD, data = TP); summary(gam_model) #GAM model
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## PB ~ s(MD) + MD
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  6.40078    1.00639    6.36 7.24e-09 ***
## MD           0.59047    0.03316   17.81  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##          edf Ref.df     F p-value    
## s(MD) 0.7416 0.7416 54.35  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Rank: 10/11
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.111   Deviance explained =   12%
## GCV = 384.18  Scale est. = 376.17    n = 96

<– Hypothesis H2 –>

# H2 : Le niveau d'investissement digital influence positivement le PBR (Price-to-Book Ratio).
rh2 <- lm(PBR ~ InvestissementDigital + ROE, TP); summary(rh2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PBR ~ InvestissementDigital + ROE, data = TP)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.4103 -0.9991 -0.2077  0.6934  4.3296 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)           -0.15397    0.44847  -0.343 0.732201    
## InvestissementDigital  0.41327    0.11922   3.467 0.000829 ***
## ROE                    0.15322    0.01719   8.914 8.07e-14 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1.417 on 85 degrees of freedom
##   (8 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.535,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.5241 
## F-statistic:  48.9 on 2 and 85 DF,  p-value: 7.34e-15
par(mfrow = c(2, 2)); plot(rh2)

diag_data <- augment(rh2)
ggplot(diag_data, aes(.fitted, .resid)) + geom_point() + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") + theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Résidus vs valeurs ajustées", x = "Valeurs ajustées", y = "Résidus")  # Résidus vs valeurs ajustées

ggplot(diag_data, aes(.fitted, sqrt(abs(.resid)))) + geom_point() + theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Échelle des résidus (homoscédasticité)", x = "Valeurs ajustées", y = "√|Résidus|") # Homoscédasticité : Résidus vs valeurs ajustées

ggplot(diag_data, aes(sample = .resid)) + stat_qq() + stat_qq_line(color = "red") + theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "QQ-plot des résidus", x = "Quantiles théoriques", y = "Quantiles des résidus") # QQ-plot pour tester la normalité des résidus

rh2log <- lm(log(PBR+0.01) ~ InvestissementDigital + log(ROE+0.01), TP); summary(rh2log)
## Warning in log(ROE + 0.01): NaNs produced
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = log(PBR + 0.01) ~ InvestissementDigital + log(ROE + 
##     0.01), data = TP)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.2940 -0.3299  0.0124  0.3753  3.3576 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)           -0.57185    0.23122  -2.473  0.01546 *  
## InvestissementDigital  0.17118    0.05836   2.933  0.00435 ** 
## log(ROE + 0.01)        0.41486    0.05904   7.027  5.7e-10 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.665 on 82 degrees of freedom
##   (11 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.4368, Adjusted R-squared:  0.4231 
## F-statistic:  31.8 on 2 and 82 DF,  p-value: 5.99e-11
diag_data <- augment(rh2log)
ggplot(diag_data, aes(.fitted, .resid)) + geom_point() + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") + theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Résidus vs valeurs ajustées", x = "Valeurs ajustées", y = "Résidus") # Résidus vs valeurs ajustées

ggplot(diag_data, aes(.fitted, sqrt(abs(.resid)))) + geom_point() + theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Échelle des résidus (homoscédasticité)", x = "Valeurs ajustées", y = "√|Résidus|") # Homoscédasticité : Résidus vs valeurs ajustées

ggplot(diag_data, aes(sample = .resid)) + stat_qq() + stat_qq_line(color = "red") + theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "QQ-plot des résidus", x = "Quantiles théoriques", y = "Quantiles des résidus") # QQ-plot pour tester la normalité des résidus

bptest(rh2log); coeftest(rh2log, vcov = vcovHC(rh2log, type = "HC3"))
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  rh2log
## BP = 55.356, df = 2, p-value = 9.543e-13
## 
## t test of coefficients:
## 
##                        Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept)           -0.571853   0.962792 -0.5940 0.554178   
## InvestissementDigital  0.171179   0.064342  2.6605 0.009384 **
## log(ROE + 0.01)        0.414859   0.365132  1.1362 0.259187   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
poolingsh2 <- plm(PBR ~ InvestissementDigital + ROE, pdata, model = "pooling"); summary(poolingsh2)
## Pooling Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PBR ~ InvestissementDigital + ROE, data = pdata, 
##     model = "pooling")
## 
## Unbalanced Panel: n = 30, T = 2-3, N = 88
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -2.41032 -0.99908 -0.20775  0.69344  4.32963 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                        Estimate Std. Error t-value  Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)           -0.153971   0.448467 -0.3433  0.732201    
## InvestissementDigital  0.413274   0.119216  3.4666  0.000829 ***
## ROE                    0.153221   0.017189  8.9138 8.071e-14 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    366.9
## Residual Sum of Squares: 170.6
## R-Squared:      0.53503
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.52409
## F-statistic: 48.903 on 2 and 85 DF, p-value: 7.3403e-15
bp_test <- bptest(poolingsh2); print(bp_test) #Test d'Homoscédasticité
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  poolingsh2
## BP = 3.3108, df = 2, p-value = 0.191
bg_test <- pbgtest(poolingsh2); print(bg_test) #Test d'Autocorrélation
## 
##  Breusch-Godfrey/Wooldridge test for serial correlation in panel models
## 
## data:  PBR ~ InvestissementDigital + ROE
## chisq = 11.48, df = 2, p-value = 0.003214
## alternative hypothesis: serial correlation in idiosyncratic errors
robust_coef_cluster <- coeftest(poolingsh2, vcov = vcovHC(poolingsh2, type = "HC3", cluster = "group")); print(robust_coef_cluster)
## 
## t test of coefficients:
## 
##                        Estimate Std. Error t value  Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)           -0.153971   0.568437 -0.2709    0.7872    
## InvestissementDigital  0.413274   0.164706  2.5092    0.0140 *  
## ROE                    0.153221   0.025375  6.0382 3.965e-08 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

<– Hypothesis H3 –>

# H3 : Les dividendes versés sont plus élevés dans les entreprises cotées digitalisées
poolingH3 <- plm(log(Dividendes+0.01) ~ MD + collaborateurs, pdata, model = "pooling"); summary(poolingH3)
## Pooling Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = log(Dividendes + 0.01) ~ MD + collaborateurs, data = pdata, 
##     model = "pooling")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -7.19994 -0.85319  0.48509  1.45664  4.86832 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate  Std. Error t-value  Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)    -4.0945e-01  5.4298e-01 -0.7541 0.4527047    
## MD              3.7915e-02  9.4367e-03  4.0179 0.0001191 ***
## collaborateurs  4.9553e-05  1.3171e-04  0.3762 0.7076117    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    798.12
## Residual Sum of Squares: 662.65
## R-Squared:      0.16974
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.15188
## F-statistic: 9.50622 on 2 and 93 DF, p-value: 0.00017522
betweensh3 <- update(poolingH3, model = "between"); summary(betweensh3)
## Oneway (individual) effect Between Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = log(Dividendes + 0.01) ~ MD + collaborateurs, data = pdata, 
##     model = "between")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## Observations used in estimation: 32
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -6.22924 -0.36628  0.25454  1.08246  4.94467 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate  Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)    -7.4045e-01  9.7924e-01 -0.7561  0.45566  
## MD              4.5207e-02  1.7745e-02  2.5476  0.01641 *
## collaborateurs  2.5234e-05  2.2312e-04  0.1131  0.91074  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    242.39
## Residual Sum of Squares: 192.55
## R-Squared:      0.20565
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.15086
## F-statistic: 3.75383 on 2 and 29 DF, p-value: 0.035498
withinsh3 <- update(poolingH3, model = "within"); summary(withinsh3)
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = log(Dividendes + 0.01) ~ MD + collaborateurs, data = pdata, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min.    1st Qu.     Median    3rd Qu.       Max. 
## -4.9933548 -0.1325335  0.0011875  0.1572660  3.9759403 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate  Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)
## MD              1.1092e-02  8.3805e-03  1.3235   0.1905
## collaborateurs -8.6960e-06  5.0402e-04 -0.0173   0.9863
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    70.944
## Residual Sum of Squares: 68.818
## R-Squared:      0.029962
## Adj. R-Squared: -0.48635
## F-statistic: 0.957505 on 2 and 62 DF, p-value: 0.38945
randomsh3 <- update(poolingH3, model = "random"); summary(randomsh3)
## Oneway (individual) effect Random Effect Model 
##    (Swamy-Arora's transformation)
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = log(Dividendes + 0.01) ~ MD + collaborateurs, data = pdata, 
##     model = "random")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Effects:
##                 var std.dev share
## idiosyncratic 1.110   1.054  0.15
## individual    6.270   2.504  0.85
## theta: 0.7639
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -5.05441 -0.16992  0.18837  0.48929  2.88169 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error z-value Pr(>|z|)  
## (Intercept)    0.59140363 0.64871665  0.9117  0.36195  
## MD             0.01656770 0.00747487  2.2165  0.02666 *
## collaborateurs 0.00010308 0.00020007  0.5152  0.60639  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    111.47
## Residual Sum of Squares: 104.63
## R-Squared:      0.061329
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.041142
## Chisq: 6.07623 on 2 DF, p-value: 0.047925
pFtest(withinsh3, poolingH3)
## 
##  F test for individual effects
## 
## data:  log(Dividendes + 0.01) ~ MD + collaborateurs
## F = 17.258, df1 = 31, df2 = 62, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: significant effects
phtest(withinsh3, randomsh3)
## 
##  Hausman Test
## 
## data:  log(Dividendes + 0.01) ~ MD + collaborateurs
## chisq = 3.4845, df = 2, p-value = 0.1751
## alternative hypothesis: one model is inconsistent
tobitsh3 <- tobit(log(Dividendes) ~ MD + DividendYield, data = pdata, left = 0); summary(tobitsh3) # Modèle Tobit pour données censurées (Dividendes >= 0)
## 
## Call:
## tobit(formula = log(Dividendes) ~ MD + DividendYield, left = 0, 
##     data = pdata)
## 
## Observations:
##          Total  Left-censored     Uncensored Right-censored 
##             96             17             79              0 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   -0.228295   0.400299  -0.570    0.568    
## MD             0.020742   0.004983   4.162 3.15e-05 ***
## DividendYield  0.475918   0.081455   5.843 5.14e-09 ***
## Log(scale)     0.335006   0.082709   4.050 5.11e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Scale: 1.398 
## 
## Gaussian distribution
## Number of Newton-Raphson Iterations: 4 
## Log-likelihood: -152.7 on 4 Df
## Wald-statistic: 48.71 on 2 Df, p-value: 2.6416e-11
# Analyse de discontinuité pour les seuils de maturité digitale
rdplot(log(pdata$Dividendes+0.01), pdata$MD, c = quantile(pdata$MD, 0.75), title = "Effet de seuil de maturite digitale",
       x.label = "Score MD", y.label = "Dividend")
## [1] "Mass points detected in the running variable."

<– Hypothesis H4 –>

# H4 : L’impact de la MD sur la PB varie selon les secteurs
poolingH4 <- plm(PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur)+ Age + collaborateurs, pdata, model = "pooling"); summary(poolingH4)
## Pooling Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + collaborateurs, 
##     data = pdata, model = "pooling")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## -21.71247  -2.95134   0.36749   4.36417  29.06793 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                                           Estimate
## (Intercept)                                                             41.0694742
## MD                                                                      -0.0274345
## factor(Secteur)Assurances                                               11.4502950
## factor(Secteur)Banques                                                   8.2942016
## factor(Secteur)Batiment et Materiaux de Construction                     7.3624774
## factor(Secteur)Boissons                                                -20.0527075
## factor(Secteur)Chimie                                                  -18.2545258
## factor(Secteur)Distributeurs                                           -12.7490803
## factor(Secteur)Electricité                                              11.4246376
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Agricole                                      -13.6251933
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Pharmaceutique                                 -6.9170988
## factor(Secteur)Ingénieries et Biens d'Equipement Industriels           -32.7053523
## factor(Secteur)Loisirs et Hôtels                                       -24.9083073
## factor(Secteur)Matériels, Logiciels et Services Informatiques            1.9348400
## factor(Secteur)Mines                                                     1.1247946
## factor(Secteur)Participation et promotion immobilières                 -15.3843770
## factor(Secteur)Pétrole et Gaz                                           24.3479157
## factor(Secteur)Santé                                                   -37.4609988
## factor(Secteur)Services de transport                                   -12.2567447
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de financement et Autres Activités Financières -10.2666086
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de placement immobilier                         -8.9382964
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de Portefeuilles / Holdings                    -30.3941771
## factor(Secteur)Sylviculture et Papier                                  -11.2666774
## factor(Secteur)Télécommunications                                       16.4992417
## factor(Secteur)Transport                                                -3.1031845
## Age                                                                     -0.1440491
## collaborateurs                                                           0.0059358
##                                                                         Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                                              9.9656586
## MD                                                                       0.0696253
## factor(Secteur)Assurances                                                6.8896478
## factor(Secteur)Banques                                                   7.5434065
## factor(Secteur)Batiment et Materiaux de Construction                     7.1112840
## factor(Secteur)Boissons                                                  7.9231540
## factor(Secteur)Chimie                                                    8.8529351
## factor(Secteur)Distributeurs                                             7.0801677
## factor(Secteur)Electricité                                              10.6050648
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Agricole                                       11.5193112
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Pharmaceutique                                  9.4788120
## factor(Secteur)Ingénieries et Biens d'Equipement Industriels             9.4632988
## factor(Secteur)Loisirs et Hôtels                                         9.7825470
## factor(Secteur)Matériels, Logiciels et Services Informatiques            9.5734332
## factor(Secteur)Mines                                                     6.6074345
## factor(Secteur)Participation et promotion immobilières                   9.4179184
## factor(Secteur)Pétrole et Gaz                                            8.6688351
## factor(Secteur)Santé                                                    11.3931129
## factor(Secteur)Services de transport                                    10.4025782
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de financement et Autres Activités Financières   8.2902069
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de placement immobilier                          9.5678867
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de Portefeuilles / Holdings                      9.3913245
## factor(Secteur)Sylviculture et Papier                                    8.3200999
## factor(Secteur)Télécommunications                                       10.7844587
## factor(Secteur)Transport                                                 8.0104228
## Age                                                                      0.0993050
## collaborateurs                                                           0.0009814
##                                                                        t-value
## (Intercept)                                                             4.1211
## MD                                                                     -0.3940
## factor(Secteur)Assurances                                               1.6620
## factor(Secteur)Banques                                                  1.0995
## factor(Secteur)Batiment et Materiaux de Construction                    1.0353
## factor(Secteur)Boissons                                                -2.5309
## factor(Secteur)Chimie                                                  -2.0620
## factor(Secteur)Distributeurs                                           -1.8007
## factor(Secteur)Electricité                                              1.0773
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Agricole                                      -1.1828
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Pharmaceutique                                -0.7297
## factor(Secteur)Ingénieries et Biens d'Equipement Industriels           -3.4560
## factor(Secteur)Loisirs et Hôtels                                       -2.5462
## factor(Secteur)Matériels, Logiciels et Services Informatiques           0.2021
## factor(Secteur)Mines                                                    0.1702
## factor(Secteur)Participation et promotion immobilières                 -1.6335
## factor(Secteur)Pétrole et Gaz                                           2.8087
## factor(Secteur)Santé                                                   -3.2880
## factor(Secteur)Services de transport                                   -1.1782
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de financement et Autres Activités Financières -1.2384
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de placement immobilier                        -0.9342
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de Portefeuilles / Holdings                    -3.2364
## factor(Secteur)Sylviculture et Papier                                  -1.3542
## factor(Secteur)Télécommunications                                       1.5299
## factor(Secteur)Transport                                               -0.3874
## Age                                                                    -1.4506
## collaborateurs                                                          6.0483
##                                                                         Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)                                                            0.0001033
## MD                                                                     0.6947736
## factor(Secteur)Assurances                                              0.1010585
## factor(Secteur)Banques                                                 0.2753585
## factor(Secteur)Batiment et Materiaux de Construction                   0.3041329
## factor(Secteur)Boissons                                                0.0136609
## factor(Secteur)Chimie                                                  0.0429785
## factor(Secteur)Distributeurs                                           0.0761244
## factor(Secteur)Electricité                                             0.2851066

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    40190
## Residual Sum of Squares: 8276.3
## R-Squared:      0.79407
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.71648
## F-statistic: 10.2334 on 26 and 69 DF, p-value: 6.538e-15
betweenh4 <- update(poolingH4, model = "between"); summary(betweenh4)
## Oneway (individual) effect Between Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + collaborateurs, 
##     data = pdata, model = "between")

## Age

## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    11879
## Residual Sum of Squares: 1162.6
## R-Squared:      0.90212
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.39317
## F-statistic: 1.77252 on 26 and 5 DF, p-value: 0.27351
withinh4 <- update(poolingH4, model = "within"); summary(withinh4)
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + collaborateurs, 
##     data = pdata, model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## -25.03250  -2.14971  -0.29184   2.51785  25.61959 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)
## MD             0.0545005  0.0676459  0.8057   0.4235
## collaborateurs 0.0011825  0.0040683  0.2906   0.7723
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    4553.6
## Residual Sum of Squares: 4483.8
## R-Squared:      0.015327
## Adj. R-Squared: -0.50877
## F-statistic: 0.482518 on 2 and 62 DF, p-value: 0.61953
randomh4 <- update(poolingH4, model = "random"); summary(randomh4)
## Oneway (individual) effect Random Effect Model 
##    (Swamy-Arora's transformation)
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + collaborateurs, 
##     data = pdata, model = "random")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Effects:
##                   var std.dev share
## idiosyncratic  72.319   8.504 0.258
## individual    208.421  14.437 0.742
## theta: 0.678
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## -24.28392  -2.70505  -0.26859   2.63685  26.94964

## Age                                                                     0.42937
## collaborateurs                                                          0.01743
##                                                                         
## (Intercept)                                                            .
## MD                                                                      
## factor(Secteur)Assurances                                               
## factor(Secteur)Banques                                                  
## factor(Secteur)Batiment et Materiaux de Construction                    
## factor(Secteur)Boissons                                                 
## factor(Secteur)Chimie                                                   
## factor(Secteur)Distributeurs                                            
## factor(Secteur)Electricité                                              
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Agricole                                       
## factor(Secteur)Industrie Pharmaceutique                                 
## factor(Secteur)Ingénieries et Biens d'Equipement Industriels            
## factor(Secteur)Loisirs et Hôtels                                        
## factor(Secteur)Matériels, Logiciels et Services Informatiques           
## factor(Secteur)Mines                                                    
## factor(Secteur)Participation et promotion immobilières                  
## factor(Secteur)Pétrole et Gaz                                           
## factor(Secteur)Santé                                                    
## factor(Secteur)Services de transport                                    
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de financement et Autres Activités Financières  
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de placement immobilier                         
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de Portefeuilles / Holdings                     
## factor(Secteur)Sylviculture et Papier                                   
## factor(Secteur)Télécommunications                                       
## factor(Secteur)Transport                                                
## Age                                                                     
## collaborateurs                                                         *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    8248
## Residual Sum of Squares: 4947.3
## R-Squared:      0.40018
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.17416
## Chisq: 46.0339 on 26 DF, p-value: 0.0090426
pFtest(withinh4,poolingH4 )
## 
##  F test for individual effects
## 
## data:  PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + collaborateurs
## F = 7.4916, df1 = 7, df2 = 62, p-value = 1.612e-06
## alternative hypothesis: significant effects
phtest(withinh4, randomh4)
## 
##  Hausman Test
## 
## data:  PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + collaborateurs
## chisq = 1.3444, df = 2, p-value = 0.5106
## alternative hypothesis: one model is inconsistent
dynamicH4 <- dynlm(PB ~ L(MD, 1) + factor(Secteur) + Age, data = pdata); summary(dynamicH4) # modele dynamique
## 
## Time series regression with "numeric" data:
## Start = 1, End = 96
## 
## Call:
## dynlm(formula = PB ~ L(MD, 1) + factor(Secteur) + Age, data = pdata)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -30.548  -3.907  -0.048   3.842  37.202 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                                         Estimate
## (Intercept)                                                             55.13908
## L(MD, 1)                                                                 0.13814

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 13.45 on 70 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.6849, Adjusted R-squared:  0.5724 
## F-statistic: 6.086 on 25 and 70 DF,  p-value: 1.118e-09
polyH4 <- lm(PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + poly(year, 2),data = pdata); summary(polyH4) # polynomiale
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur) + Age + poly(year, 2), 
##     data = pdata)

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 13.31 on 68 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.7002, Adjusted R-squared:  0.5812 
## F-statistic: 5.883 on 27 and 68 DF,  p-value: 1.658e-09
tobitH4 <- tobit(PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur), data = pdata, left = 0); summary(tobitH4) # model tobit
## 
## Call:
## tobit(formula = PB ~ MD + factor(Secteur), left = 0, data = pdata)
##

##             96              1             95              0

## factor(Secteur)Ingénieries et Biens d'Equipement Industriels           ***
## factor(Secteur)Loisirs et Hôtels                                       *  
## factor(Secteur)Matériels, Logiciels et Services Informatiques             
## factor(Secteur)Mines                                                   .  
## factor(Secteur)Participation et promotion immobilières                    
## factor(Secteur)Pétrole et Gaz                                             
## factor(Secteur)Santé                                                   *  
## factor(Secteur)Services de transport                                      
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de financement et Autres Activités Financières .  
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de placement immobilier                           
## factor(Secteur)Sociétés de Portefeuilles / Holdings                       
## factor(Secteur)Sylviculture et Papier                                     
## factor(Secteur)Télécommunications                                      ***
## factor(Secteur)Transport                                                  
## Log(scale)                                                             ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Scale: 12.07 
## 
## Gaussian distribution
## Number of Newton-Raphson Iterations: 6 
## Log-likelihood: -372.6 on 26 Df
## Wald-statistic: 182.7 on 24 Df, p-value: < 2.22e-16

<– Hypothesis H5 –>

#   H5 : Les entreprises anciennes bénéficient moins de la digitalisation que les jeunes.
poolregh5 <- plm(PB ~ MD + Age + MD*Age, pdata, model = "pooling"); summary(poolregh5)
## Pooling Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + Age + MD * Age, data = pdata, model = "pooling")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -27.5582 -13.1152  -3.8597   9.3000  55.6232 
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept) 13.5836193  9.9070903  1.3711  0.17368  
## MD           0.3482003  0.1568842  2.2195  0.02891 *
## Age          0.2078251  0.1693343  1.2273  0.22284  
## MD:Age      -0.0022069  0.0026005 -0.8486  0.39828  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    40190
## Residual Sum of Squares: 34570
## R-Squared:      0.13983
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.11178
## F-statistic: 4.98528 on 3 and 92 DF, p-value: 0.0029995
betweenreg5 <- update(poolregh5, model = "between"); summary(betweenreg5)
## Oneway (individual) effect Between Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + Age + MD * Age, data = pdata, model = "between")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## Observations used in estimation: 32
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -23.3329 -12.7909  -3.3099   6.0222  47.4062 
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)  6.9194268 19.8196478  0.3491   0.7296
## MD           0.4694020  0.3219474  1.4580   0.1560
## Age          0.2920279  0.3442456  0.8483   0.4035
## MD:Age      -0.0036587  0.0054259 -0.6743   0.5057
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    11879
## Residual Sum of Squares: 9757.2
## R-Squared:      0.1786
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.090595
## F-statistic: 2.0294 on 3 and 28 DF, p-value: 0.13254
withinreg5 <- update(poolregh5, model = "within"); summary(withinreg5)
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + Age + MD * Age, data = pdata, model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -24.8784  -2.1514  -0.4530   2.5320  25.4652 
## 
## Coefficients:
##          Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)
## MD     0.04409120 0.12194921  0.3616   0.7189
## MD:Age 0.00030073 0.00189991  0.1583   0.8747
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    4553.6
## Residual Sum of Squares: 4488.1
## R-Squared:      0.014383
## Adj. R-Squared: -0.51022
## F-statistic: 0.452385 on 2 and 62 DF, p-value: 0.63819
randomreg5 <- update(poolregh5, model = "random"); summary(randomreg5)
## Oneway (individual) effect Random Effect Model 
##    (Swamy-Arora's transformation)
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = PB ~ MD + Age + MD * Age, data = pdata, model = "random")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 32, T = 3, N = 96
## 
## Effects:
##                   var std.dev share
## idiosyncratic  72.388   8.508 0.182
## individual    324.342  18.010 0.818
## theta: 0.7369
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -23.5608  -4.6512  -1.5247   3.0067  27.4902 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                Estimate  Std. Error z-value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept) 26.04098087  9.45890653  2.7531 0.005904 **
## MD           0.11228489  0.11371202  0.9874 0.323422   
## Age          0.09167069  0.15182518  0.6038 0.545983   
## MD:Age      -0.00011646  0.00180042 -0.0647 0.948425   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    7021.1
## Residual Sum of Squares: 6714
## R-Squared:      0.043748
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.012565
## Chisq: 4.20891 on 3 DF, p-value: 0.23977
pFtest(withinreg5,poolregh5)
## 
##  F test for individual effects
## 
## data:  PB ~ MD + Age + MD * Age
## F = 13.852, df1 = 30, df2 = 62, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: significant effects
tobitH5 <- censReg(PB ~ MD + Age, data = pdata); summary(tobitH5)
## 
## Call:
## censReg(formula = PB ~ MD + Age, data = pdata)
## 
## Observations:
##          Total  Left-censored     Uncensored Right-censored 
##             96              1             95              0 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. error t value  Pr(> t)    
## (Intercept) 20.39155    5.11393   3.987 6.68e-05 ***
## MD           0.23251    0.06503   3.575  0.00035 ***
## Age          0.07470    0.06287   1.188  0.23481    
## logSigma     2.95556    0.07272  40.644  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Newton-Raphson maximisation, 6 iterations
## Return code 1: gradient close to zero (gradtol)
## Log-likelihood: -416.7491 on 4 Df
dynH5 <- dynlm(PB ~ L(MD, 1) + Age, data = pdata); summary(dynH5)
## 
## Time series regression with "numeric" data:
## Start = 1, End = 96
## 
## Call:
## dynlm(formula = PB ~ L(MD, 1) + Age, data = pdata)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -26.543 -13.058  -3.981   8.530  54.569 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 20.76650    5.14131   4.039 0.000110 ***
## L(MD, 1)     0.22720    0.06535   3.477 0.000774 ***
## Age          0.07458    0.06334   1.178 0.241994    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 19.36 on 93 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1331, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1145 
## F-statistic: 7.139 on 2 and 93 DF,  p-value: 0.001305
model_gam5 <- gam(PB ~ s(MD, by=Age), data = pdata); summary(model_gam5) # Test de Non-Linéarité :
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## PB ~ s(MD, by = Age)
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   33.481      4.305   7.777 9.89e-12 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##           edf Ref.df     F p-value  
## s(MD):Age   2      2 4.812  0.0103 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.0743   Deviance explained = 9.38%
## GCV = 404.26  Scale est. = 391.62    n = 96
model_cox <- coxph(Surv(Age, ifelse(pdata$AdoptionTech > median(pdata$AdoptionTech), 1, 0)) ~  MD, data = pdata);
summary(model_cox) # Analyse de Survie pour l'Adoption Effective
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(Age, ifelse(pdata$AdoptionTech > median(pdata$AdoptionTech), 
##     1, 0)) ~ MD, data = pdata)
## 
##   n= 96, number of events= 36 
## 
##       coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## MD 0.06010   1.06195  0.01084 5.544 2.95e-08 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##    exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## MD     1.062     0.9417      1.04     1.085
## 
## Concordance= 0.843  (se = 0.033 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 49.58  on 1 df,   p=2e-12
## Wald test            = 30.74  on 1 df,   p=3e-08
## Score (logrank) test = 39.93  on 1 df,   p=3e-10
ggsurvplot(survfit(model_cox),  data = pdata, risk.table = TRUE) # Courbe de Kaplan-Meier
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Strata"

model_gam5 <- gam(PB ~ s(MD, by=Age) + MD + Age, data = pdata); summary(model_gam5)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## PB ~ s(MD, by = Age) + MD + Age
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.009179   0.001068   8.593 1.93e-13 ***
## MD          0.542722   0.067079   8.091 2.19e-12 ***
## Age         0.075671   0.026789   2.825  0.00579 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##           edf Ref.df     F  p-value    
## s(MD):Age 1.5    1.5 8.872 0.000687 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Rank: 11/13
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.104   Deviance explained = 12.2%
## GCV = 391.54  Scale est. = 379.31    n = 96
data_grid <- expand.grid(MD = seq(min(pdata$MD), max(pdata$MD), length.out = 100), Age = quantile(pdata$Age, probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9)))
ggplot(data_grid, aes(x = MD, y = predict(model_gam5, newdata = data_grid), color = factor(Age))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) + scale_color_viridis( discrete = TRUE, name = "Age de l'entreprise",
    labels = c("Jeune (10e percentile)", "Moyen (50e percentile)", "Agee (90e percentile)")) +
  labs(title = "Effet interactif de la maturité digitale (MD) et de l'age sur la performance boursiere (PB)",
    x = "Score de maturite digitale (MD)", y = "Performance boursiere prédite (PB)",
    caption = "Les lignes representent la relation MD-PB à différents niveaux d'age.") +theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),legend.position = "bottom")

<– Digital Manager Analysis –>

# responsable digitale 
dyn <- dynlm(PB ~ ResponsableDigitale + collaborateurs, data = pdata); summary(dyn)
## 
## Time series regression with "numeric" data:
## Start = 1, End = 96
## 
## Call:
## dynlm(formula = PB ~ ResponsableDigitale + collaborateurs, data = pdata)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -30.717  -8.843  -1.579   8.505  35.101 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                      Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         2.133e+01  2.710e+00   7.869 6.35e-12 ***
## ResponsableDigitale 1.004e+01  3.489e+00   2.878  0.00496 ** 
## collaborateurs      5.087e-03  7.769e-04   6.548 3.18e-09 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 15.7 on 93 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.4297, Adjusted R-squared:  0.4174 
## F-statistic: 35.03 on 2 and 93 DF,  p-value: 4.567e-12
ggplot(pdata, aes(x = ResponsableDigitale, y = PB)) + geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + geom_jitter(width = 0.1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Relation entre Responsable Digital et Performance Boursière",
       x = "Présence d'un Responsable Digital (0 = Non, 1 = Oui)",
       y = "Performance Boursière (PB)") + theme_minimal()

ggplot(pdata, aes(x = factor(ResponsableDigitale), y = PB)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Distribution de la Performance Boursière selon la Présence d'un Responsable Digital",
       x = "Présence d'un Responsable Digital (0 = Non, 1 = Oui)",
       y = "Performance Boursière (PB)") + theme_minimal()

ggplot(pdata, aes(x = ResponsableDigitale, y = PB)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Effet d'un Responsable Digital sur la Performance Boursière",
       x = "Présence d'un Responsable Digital (0 = Non, 1 = Oui)",
       y = "Performance Boursière (PB)") + theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(pdata, aes(x = collaborateurs, y = PB, color = factor(ResponsableDigitale))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue"), labels = c("Sans Responsable Digital", "Avec Responsable Digital")) +
  labs(title = "Relation entre Collaborateurs, Responsable Digital et Performance Boursière",
       x = "Nombre de Collaborateurs",
       y = "Performance Boursière (PB)",
       color = "Présence d'un Responsable Digital") + theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
